TextENGNew: The following day.
changeReason: Time reference changes from 'evening' to 'day', altering when events occur.

TextENGNew: We gathered at the Sonozaki residence that night.
I had imagined we'd be sitting around Mion's grandmother, chatting over tea or something.
...But this was nothing so casual.

Mion's grandmother -- known by the fearsome title 'Sonozaki Myouou' -- remained in her futon,@k propped up against a large cushion-like support, her upper body raised, and her mere presence made me feel as though I stood before some feudal lord.
changeReason: Authority metaphor changed from 'queen' to 'feudal lord', shifting cultural framing.

TextENGNew: That wasn't all.
...About five of the highest-ranking Sonozaki family elders sat arrayed around her, and the atmosphere was less a neighborhood council meeting and more a yakuza summit.
Yes, this was no town council discussion.
This was a meeting of the Three Families, with all three heads assembled.
I felt painfully out of place, but... this was no time to be intimidated.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: If I could convince this old woman here, I could rally the full might of the village to save Satoko...!

``......So that's where things stand.@k@vS12/18/183300082. If the town council doesn't change its current stance, the center won't listen to a word Keiichi-kun says, no matter what he tries.@k@vS12/18/183300083. At the very least, the council needs to declare its neutrality, I believe...``
The mayor spoke on our behalf to open the discussion.
...The old woman's face was stern as she listened, though I couldn't tell whether her expression was always that severe or whether the topic had made it so.
She murmured something in a voice so low only those closest could hear, and a woman in a kimono spoke in her stead.@k ...That must be Mion's mother.

``If there had been some procedural error at the Okinomiya child consultation center, that would be one matter, but they are following proper protocol.@k@vS12/16/182200002. Not only that, they have promised to take Keiichi-kun's passion into account.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Given that, what complaint does the town council have?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Mom.@k@vS12/03/180300595. Let me explain this part.@k@vS12/03/180300596. Satoko's uncle is an absolute lowlife.``
``Kasai, you looked into him, right?@k@vS12/06/180600367. Houjou Teppei, I mean.``
``...Yes. I have already reported my findings to Akane-san.``

``So there you have it, Mom.@k@vS12/06/SHIO082201. If we leave it to the center and their careful approach, it'll probably be months before they step in.@k@vS12/06/SHIO082202. I can't leave Satoko suffering for months!``
``.........She's not going to die from a few months.@k@vS12/17/182300002. Making such a fuss over so little.``
The old woman finally deigned to address us directly, but... her words were anything but gentle.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``W-Well, she might not die, but--``
``Then shut your trap, you fool!!@k@vS12/17/182300004. Carrying on like that when you're not even putting your life on the line!!``
``You don't have the guts, you numbskull!!@k@vS12/17/182300006. What a load of nonsense!!@k@vS12/17/182300007. The more I listen, the more ridiculous it gets!!!``
...Wow.
No wonder the whole town council was terrified of her.
So this was Sonozaki Oryou -- the one they called the Grand Matriarch, the Empress of the Sonozakis.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``......Oryou.@k@vS12/05/180500871. Satoko is my friend...@k@vS12/05/180500872. Please help her...``
``Rika-chama, I've told you time and again.@k@vS12/17/182300009. You need to choose your friends more carefully.@k@vS12/17/182300010. That wretched Houjou brat is nothing but bad luck!!!``
``Even Dr. Irie has come along to show his face -- what is this nonsense!!@k@vS12/17/182300012. A man your age should know better -- is this how you repay what I've done for you?!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Oryou-san.@k@vS12/10/181000307. Satoko-chan merely happens to have been born into the Houjou family.@k@vS12/10/181000308. She herself never supported the dam project or opposed the village. Don't you think it's unfair to blame the child for the sins of her parents...?``
``What utter drivel!!!@k@vS12/17/182300014. Do you have any idea who you're running your mouth at, you insolent fool!!!``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Mii-chan, are you sure your grandmother has really forgiven Satoko-chan?``
``That's what Mion told me, anyway.@k@vS12/01/180101422. I'm starting to see why the town council people were so reluctant.``
``...You look like you have something to say, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/16/182200005. Go ahead and say it, whatever it is.``
Mion's mother fixed her gaze on me.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...She possessed a refined beauty, yet those same hawk-like eyes as Empress Sonozaki gleamed behind them.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: I see... She was unmistakably Mion's mother.
``I'm happy to speak up.@k@vS12/01/180101424. ...But is that really all right?``
``By all means.@k@vS12/16/182200007. You've come all the way to the Sonozaki main house, after all.@k@vS12/16/182200008. Speak your mind.``
Rena gave me a nod.@k ...All right.
``Sonozaki Oryou-san.@k@vS12/01/180101426. My name is Maebara Keiichi.@k@vS12/01/180101427. I only recently moved here, but I'm pleased to meet you.``
``......You don't need to introduce yourself. I know plenty about you already.``
``I'll tell you this now.@k@vS12/03/180300599. ...When the Maebara family moved in, you never came to pay your respects to Granny...@k@vS12/03/180300600. She didn't take kindly to that...``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: It wasn't exactly helpful to learn that now.
I'd just have to count my introduction today as better late than never.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Whether or not she's a Houjou isn't the issue.@k@vS12/01/180101429. A member of this village is in a critical situation right now.@k@vS12/01/180101430. The only way to save her is to appeal to the consultation center.``
``And this is something I'm doing of my own accord.@k@vS12/01/180101432. It isn't causing you any trouble.``
``Didn't the mayor explain to you that it is causing trouble?@k@vS12/16/182200010. The Hinamizawa United Town Council has an understanding with the Shishibone city government.``
``The mayor and the Grand Matriarch have agreed to let bygones be bygones and work together going forward.@k@vS12/16/182200012. That's only natural if we want this village to prosper.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...And this village's prosperity is worth sacrificing one innocent girl?``

``Did you not hear what I just said?@k@vS12/16/182200014. Nobody is sacrificing anyone.@k@vS12/16/182200015. The consultation center is handling the situation properly.@k@vS12/16/182200016. Wanting them to rush is nothing but selfishness on your part, isn't it?``
``The village and the government have a deal. Satoko gets ordinary treatment.@k@vS12/01/180101435. .........So no matter how dire Satoko's situation gets, it's none of your concern -- is that it?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``As Mion and the others just explained, Teppei is a dangerous man.``
``...Pardon me, but are you saying that any amount of emotional damage doesn't matter, Mion's mother?!``
``Then what exactly do you want us to do?``
``I'm appealing to the center, demanding they rescue her immediately.@k@vS12/01/KEI082202. And I intend to keep appealing until they take decisive action.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Then there's your answer.@k@vS12/16/182200019. You can keep doing what you're doing.@k@vS12/16/182200020. The Sonozaki family has nothing to do with it.@k@vS12/16/182200021. Isn't that sufficient?``
...Damn it.@k She was a tough nut to crack...
When I fought the elders at the assembly hall, they were all short-tempered, so it was easy to draw them into my rhythm.
But this Akane-san, Mion's mother, was a different beast entirely.
The Sonozaki twins and their grandmother were all quick to anger, but this woman alone remained cool and composed.@k Cool, yet unmistakably hostile.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``But that's no longer possible.@k@vS12/01/180101446. The town council told the government it doesn't support what we're doing, so the consultation center won't listen to our appeals anymore.``

``That's exactly the problem, Mom...@k@vS12/03/180300602. 'Do what you want' doesn't cut it anymore.@k@vS12/03/180300603. Unless the town council clearly declares whether it supports or opposes us, we can't move forward.``
``Actually, the council has already reached a consensus.@k@vS12/06/180600373. They said they'll support us as long as Granny agrees.``
``Who the hell went and decided that behind my back!!``
``You all remember how the Houjou couple behaved during the dam war.@k@vS12/16/182200023. If you're asking whether I can forgive them, my answer is also no.``
``I actually don't know much about it... What kind of people were Satoko-chan's parents...?``
``......Her mother was like a more headstrong version of Satoko.@k@vS12/05/180500874. Her father was Teppei's older brother.@k@vS12/05/180500875. He was even stronger and scarier than Teppei.``
``Even worse than Teppei...@k@vS12/01/180101448. That's not a pleasant thought.@k@vS12/01/180101449. ...I see. Now I'm starting to understand how they clashed head-on with the Sonozaki family.``
``Basically, this old woman still hasn't cooled down from the fury they stoked in her back then.``

``Saying it where we can hear you -- you've got nerve, boy.@k@vS12/16/182200025. My daughters have told me a great deal about you.@k@vS12/16/182200026. Apparently you're quite the impressive young man.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Since I'm already pushing my luck, let me be blunt.@k@vS12/01/180101452. All I need is for the old lady in that futon to nod.@k@vS12/01/180101453. I'm not asking everyone here to join our protest.@k@vS12/01/180101454. Just a simple nod -- that's all.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``............And if she refuses?``
``Then I'll crack her skull open right here and make Mion the new head, and she'll nod for us.``
``......The nerve of this brat, mouthing off like that.@k@vS12/17/182300018. The audacity -- the sheer insolence of this whelp!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The old woman glared at me with a pressure worthy of her title as Grand Matriarch.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...Under normal circumstances, that alone would have been enough to make me weep and beg for forgiveness, but unfortunately, I couldn't afford to back down.
changeReason: New translation expands on the original with additional detail that alters reader understanding.

TextENGNew: ...Every day counted for Satoko.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...We all knew it.
We knew that in just a few more days, Satoko would be broken completely.
And when that happened, we'd shed tears of regret over our own helplessness.
If we were going to regret it anyway, then I'd rather fight with everything I had right now.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: There was no such thing as going too far when it came to this...!!
``Let me be clear.@k@vS12/01/180101458. Satoko is one of us.@k@vS12/01/180101459. I'm fighting for my friend.``

``What about you?@k@vS12/01/180101461. I've heard you were the same way.@k@vS12/01/180101462. You once rallied the entire village to fight for the sake of your fellow villagers.@k@vS12/01/180101463. Wasn't that how you defeated the dam project?``
``.........For an outsider, you certainly know a lot.``

``Hold it right there, old woman.@k@vS12/01/180101465. Who are you calling an outsider?@k@vS12/01/180101466. I'm Maebara Keiichi of Hinamizawa.@k@vS12/01/180101467. I'm fighting to save a fellow villager.@k@vS12/01/180101468. And you dare call someone doing that an outsider?``
``Oryou-san.@k@vS12/10/181000310. Maebara-kun may not have been here long, but he has thoroughly integrated himself into the village and brought a refreshing new energy. He's a promising young man.``

``......Since when did Dr. Irie start calling ill-mannered brats 'promising young men'...?@k@vS12/17/182300021. Someone, bring me tea and my medicine...@k@vS12/17/182300022. The blood's rushing to my head so fast my veins are throbbing.``
The man seated at the far end stood, briefly left the room, and returned with tea and medicine.
``If you call me ill-mannered, then allow me to do this properly.``

``...Please, Sonozaki family, lend us your support in saving Houjou Satoko.@k@vS12/01/180101471. Satoko is a member of this village.@k@vS12/01/180101472. More than that, she is my friend.@k@vS12/01/180101473. I beg of you... please help us...!!``
As I bowed my head deeply, everyone followed suit and bowed alongside me.
``Keiichi-kun, raise your head.@k@vS12/16/182200029. ...What shall we do, Mother?``
``......What shall we do, what can we do.@k@vS12/17/182300024. What exactly do you expect of me?``
``He's saying Satoko-chan is family to him.@k@vS12/16/182200031. And him stepping up for his family is only right and proper.``

``...The thing is, though.@k@vS12/16/182200033. The Sonozaki family and the Houjou family were bitterly at odds.@k@vS12/16/182200034. They even publicly humiliated the Grand Matriarch.@k@vS12/16/182200035. Asking her to simply let that go isn't something done lightly.@k@vS12/16/182200036. This is a matter of principle.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...What do you mean, 'principle'?``

``In plain terms, an expression of contrition.@k@vS12/16/182200038. However, the Houjou couple who should have offered it died in an accident.@k@vS12/16/182200039. And given the severity of what they did, the settlement wouldn't come cheap.@k@vS12/16/182200040. It wouldn't come cheap at all.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``If that principle is satisfied, would you be willing to let the anger go?``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``We live by a code of honor.@k@vS12/16/182200042. If someone bows their head to us, we have no choice but to let it pass.@k@vS12/16/182200043. But surely you're not going to make Satoko-chan bow her head, Rena-chan?``

``......I would.@k@vS12/02/180200626. I'll explain to Satoko-chan about her parents' responsibility and have her apologize.@k@vS12/02/180200627. She isn't the one who should have to, but if it would earn your forgiveness -- you would forgive her, right?``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``............I can't say whether we'd forgive her until we see it for ourselves.@k@vS12/16/182200045. But... I suppose it might register as genuine contrition.``
``How about it then, Mii-chan's grandmother?@k@vS12/02/180200629. If Satoko-chan apologized for what happened during the dam war?``
``......Pfft... Ha ha ha ha!!@k@vS12/17/182300026. Well, if she actually did that, I might consider it.@k@vS12/17/182300027. I might even think about letting it go.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Her laughter was predicated on the assumption that Satoko would never actually bow her head.
At first glance, it looked as though Rena had made a one-sided concession.@k ...But this was where Rena truly became formidable.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``But if Satoko-chan apologized and settled things on her end...``
``...then wouldn't you owe an apology as well, Grandmother? For all the years you harbored ill will toward an innocent girl who never deserved it?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``.........What did you say?``
``Satoko-chan didn't support the dam project.@k@vS12/02/180200633. She was simply born a Houjou.@k@vS12/02/180200634. And yet you've despised her for that alone.``

``In this village, you are no ordinary old woman.@k@vS12/02/180200636. You are the head of the Sonozaki family -- the foremost of the Three Families, with the power to sway the will of all Hinamizawa.@k@vS12/02/180200637. When someone of your stature publicly shows contempt for Satoko-chan, it affects the entire village.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``That was made painfully clear at the town council meeting.@k@vS12/02/180200639. Everyone is afraid of what might happen to them if you learn they've been associating with the Houjou family!``

``...Well, the Grand Matriarch is the mother of Hinamizawa, after all.@k@vS12/16/182200047. She kept us all together through the hardest years during and after the war.@k@vS12/16/182200048. It's only natural she would wield such influence.``
``You cannot claim to be unaware of that influence.@k@vS12/01/180101476. ...To borrow your earlier words... Satoko is my friend.@k@vS12/01/180101477. She is family to me.``
``...If your influence brings harm to her, then I have no choice but to fight you.@k@vS12/01/180101479. That's what it means to protect your own!``

``......Hmm.@k@vS12/16/182200050. ...For a city boy, you certainly don't mince words.@k@vS12/16/182200051. Let me ask the other family members their thoughts.@k@vS12/16/182200052. What do you all think?``
``............He has a point.@k@vS12/00/eldA18039. The dispute between the Sonozaki and Houjou families isn't his affair, but he's made his position on his own people very clear.``
``If it weren't for the Houjou name, the Sonozaki family wouldn't have any objection...``
``Let them do as they please.@k@vS12/00/eldC18018. The Sonozaki family neither helps nor hinders.@k@vS12/00/eldC18019. That should be enough.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...It appears that won't be sufficient, ladies and gentlemen.``
The largest and most intimidating man, wearing sunglasses, spoke in a deep, rumbling voice.

``He's saying the town council won't be satisfied unless the Sonozaki family gives its explicit approval.@k@vS12/00/mionF18003. And without the council's endorsement, the government won't listen.@k@vS12/00/mionF18004. ...We have no choice but to give a definitive answer.``
``What's your own opinion?``

``......Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/00/mionF18006. He's a far more impressive young man than my daughters' stories had led me to expect.@k@vS12/00/mionF18007. Frankly, I've revised my opinion of him.@k@vS12/00/mionF18008. He's an admirable man.``
``Oh, ...th-thanks... Are you Mion's father...?``
I'd been certain he was the head of some yakuza clan, but he turned out to be Mion's father.
...Come to think of it, I'd heard that her father actually was exactly that...@k Well, that explained the overwhelming presence.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``We bear some responsibility for the trouble your friend has suffered.@k@vS12/00/mionF18010. Whatever her parents may have done, the daughter is blameless.@k@vS12/00/mionF18011. ...Wouldn't you agree, Mother?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...I couldn't care less!!@k@vS12/17/182300030. Every last wretch who bears the Houjou name is accursed!!``
``And this boy just said he'd crack my skull open, didn't he?!@k@vS12/17/182300032. Don't you dare call someone like that 'admirable'!!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...Mother.@k@vS12/00/mionF18013. Won't you please lend Keiichi-kun your support, just this once?@k@vS12/00/mionF18014. ...It's rare to find a young person with this kind of passion nowadays.@k@vS12/00/mionF18015. Not many can speak their mind this clearly.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Granny, please, I'm asking you too...@k@vS12/03/180300605. Please lend us your strength...!@k@vS12/03/180300606. Satoko is my friend as well.``
``I've always admired your stories of fighting for your fellow villagers...!@k@vS12/03/180300608. That's why I want to fight for my friend, just like you did...!``
``I bow my head as well.@k@vS12/06/180600375. Satoko is family to me.@k@vS12/06/180600376. She's like my little sister.@k@vS12/06/180600377. Please, I beg you -- lend us your strength.``
The old woman gave no answer, merely glaring at the bowing Mion and the others.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...As I feared, prying cooperation from this stubborn old woman would be no easy task...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The mayor looked at a complete loss as well.
The old woman's piercing gaze swung to me. I returned it in kind.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``I already bowed once. I won't do it again.@k@vS12/01/180101482. But I'll say it one more time.``

``Help us.@k@vS12/01/180101484. Satoko is one of my own, and she's one of your village.@k@vS12/01/180101485. She never once disrespected you.@k@vS12/01/180101486. You have no reason to bear her a grudge.``

``With the council's backing, the consultation center can't just dismiss us anymore.@k@vS12/01/180101488. If they still won't act, we'll march there again.@k@vS12/01/180101489. This time with all of Hinamizawa behind us!@k@vS12/01/180101490. Tomorrow is Saturday.@k@vS12/01/180101491. The center is open in the morning.``
``And the villagers should be gathering for festival preparations.@k@vS12/01/180101493. We'll take that entire crowd to the consultation center!``
``Th-That would be extraordinary...@k@vS12/10/181000312. We'd be talking about a hundred people or more.``
``...How many do you think you could gather?``
``Since this is on such short notice, I can't say how many would come, but...@k@vS12/01/180101495. At the very least, I'll stand before everyone and speak.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``How do things look from the town council's perspective, Mayor?``

``The council members are largely sympathetic.@k@vS12/18/183300085. ...Our relationship with the government is certainly important, but even among the officials, there's a growing sentiment that it goes against Hinamizawa's spirit to be unable to fight for Satoko-chan -- that the ideals of the Guardians demand action.``
``...The consensus is that we understand what Keiichi-kun is doing, and there's no grounds for opposition.``

``So the council has already united even without the Grand Matriarch?@k@vS12/16/182200057. And now you want the Sonozaki family to cast the final vote?@k@vS12/16/182200058. That's a rather shrewd maneuver on the council's part.``
``...Akane-san, Oryou-san.@k@vS12/18/183300088. I'll be blunt... I feel sorry for Satoko-chan.@k@vS12/18/183300089. She's been enduring it all alone, so bravely.``
``I think it's time for a proclamation -- that Satoko-chan is no longer despised by anyone in this village...``
``Absolutely not!!!@k@vS12/17/182300037. You expect me to forgive that wretched Houjou brat!!``
``Then it's settled!!@k@vS12/01/180101497. You're the enemy of my friend.@k@vS12/01/180101498. That makes you my enemy!!@k@vS12/01/180101499. I'll finish you off right here and now!!``
``Mion, take over as head of the family immediately and give us your support!!@k@vS12/01/180101501. I'll strangle this old hag to death right this instant!!!``
As I leapt to my feet to charge at her, the stern-faced relatives all stood in unison, blocking my path.
changeReason: New translation expands on the original with additional detail that alters reader understanding.

TextENGNew: ...Actually, why do some of them have their hands inside their jackets?
I really didn't want to find out what they were reaching for...
``.........AAAAARRRGGHH, you're driving me absolutely mad!!@k@vS12/17/182300039. The gall of this brat!!@k@vS12/17/182300040. Outrageous, absolutely outrageous!!!``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Oryou, you are going to burst several blood vessels.``
``Ahh, sorry, Rika-chama.@k@vS12/17/182300042. Could you pass me that pouch of medicine...?@k@vS12/17/182300043. My veins really are about to burst...``
I couldn't make out everything she said, but her fury was unmistakable.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Then, still looking thoroughly displeased, she muttered something to Mion's mother.@k Akane spoke on her behalf.
``I'm sorry, but we'll have to call it here for tonight.@k@vS12/16/182200060. The Grand Matriarch's headache has gotten severe, and she says she can't continue.``
``B-But... I still haven't gotten an answer!``

``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/16/182200062. Knowing when to come back another day is a lesson in adult etiquette you'd do well to learn.@k@vS12/16/182200063. I've already been quite generous with you today.``
``...I'm extending you this courtesy because you're friends with my daughters.@k@vS12/16/182200065. I'd appreciate it if you showed some consideration for my position...?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``K-Kei-chan... we've done enough for tonight...@k@vS12/03/180300610. Let's leave it here...``
Seeing Mion and Shion start to panic told me everything I needed to know... Even Mion's mother, who rarely lost her temper, was apparently fearsome when she did.
changeReason: New translation expands on the original with additional detail that alters reader understanding.

TextENGNew: The twins seemed to read her current expression as about ten seconds from detonation.
...But should I really back down here...?
Damn it... I so desperately wanted her to nod!
If she agreed tonight, we could mobilize in force tomorrow!
The people at the consultation center had been laughing today.
Our numbers had grown each day through the third, but plummeted on the fourth, and they'd smugly calculated that in another day or two, we'd stop coming entirely!
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: We couldn't afford to take even one day off.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: We didn't have a single day's grace!
But the meeting was adjourned.
The old woman retired to sleep, and we were all ushered out.
The younger group was shown outside while the mayor stayed behind to make his final courtesies.
We waited for him in the grand Sonozaki family garden, milling about.
``...Damn it... We were so close...``
``You said everything that needed to be said, Keiichi-kun.``
``But we have to give them time to think it over.@k@vS12/02/180200642. ...Even though we don't have time to spare, of course.``
``How did things look from your perspective, Coach?``
``...Oryou-san went into full foul-mood mode.``

``Once she gets like that, she can stay that way for three full days if you're unlucky.@k@vS12/10/181000315. ...You mentioned earlier about rallying people at the Watanagashi preparations tomorrow, Maebara-san, but I think that might be difficult now.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``......But I think she heard us out to the very end.@k@vS12/05/180500878. All we can do now is trust in Oryou...``
``You're right.@k@vS12/01/180101506. ...Trusting and waiting is the hardest thing there is.``
``...Hey, everyone. Good work in there.``
Mion and Shion came running across the garden toward us.
``Hey, you two did great. Nice job.@k@vS12/01/180101508. How did it look from your end?@k@vS12/01/180101509. Think we have a shot?``
``I have no idea.@k@vS12/03/180300613. From where I was sitting, Granny's impression couldn't have been worse.``
``...You should probably never set foot in the main house again, Kei-chan...``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``So what, I can never hang out at your place again?@k@vS12/01/180101511. Great.``

``Still... you were really something, Kei-chan.@k@vS12/06/180600379. Yesterday at the assembly hall was one thing, but standing firm against that kind of intimidation without flinching is seriously impressive.``
changeReason: New translation expands on the original with additional detail that alters reader understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Was it really that scary?@k@vS12/01/180101513. Sure, she had a fierce look on her face, but it wasn't enough to make me tremble.``
``Sigh...@k@vS12/03/180300616. That's probably because you don't know how truly terrifying Granny can be...``
``Well, ignorance can be its own kind of weapon, I guess...``
``That's not it.@k@vS12/02/180200644. Even if Keiichi-kun had known how scary she could be, he wouldn't have backed down. Not when Satoko-chan was at stake.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Come to think of it, you seemed completely unfazed as well, Ryuuguu-san, no matter how much she shouted.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``She was raising her voice, but she wasn't actually angry.@k@vS12/02/180200646. I wasn't scared at all, not at all.``
``Y-You thought she wasn't angry?!``
``Nope.@k@vS12/02/180200648. Mii-chan's grandmother wasn't angry at all.``
R-Really...??
changeReason: New translation significantly condenses the original, omitting detail.

TextENGNew: To me, she looked so furious I was half-worried a blood vessel would burst...
Just then, I heard footsteps crunching on the gravel, drawing closer.@k It was the mayor, accompanied by Mion's mother.
``Oh, Mayor Kimiyoshi...!@k@vS12/06/180600381. How did it go?@k@vS12/06/180600382. Was Granny still fuming?``
``Hahahahaha...@k@vS12/18/183300092. She said yes.``
``What?``
``The Grand Matriarch gave her approval.@k@vS12/18/183300094. She says you can do as you wish, Keiichi-kun.``
``Is that... is that really true?!``
``I-Is it really, Mom?!``
I couldn't fathom what sequence of events had led the furious old woman to grant her permission.@k ...But in the end, she had accepted us.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``The Grand Matriarch has approved the Hinamizawa United Town Council lending its support to Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/16/182200067. Having the United Council behind you is effectively the same as having the Onigafuchi Guardians behind you.``
``Which means you absolutely cannot lose.@k@vS12/16/182200069. And you cannot wage a fight that would disgrace us.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``You, a newcomer to this village, will be carrying the history of solidarity that Hinamizawa has built over decades.@k@vS12/16/182200071. Are you prepared for that weight?``

``My, an instant answer -- now that's a man!@k@vS12/16/182200073. What admirable guts.@k@vS12/16/182200074. Not many could talk to the Grand Matriarch the way you did!@k@vS12/16/182200075. I nearly had a heart attack when you lunged and grabbed her by the collar at the end!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Huh?@k@vS12/01/180101517. Well, I did almost lunge at her, but... that was, you know, more of a bluff...``
``Heh heh heh.``
``Listen carefully.@k@vS12/16/182200078. You leapt at the Grand Matriarch, grabbed her by the throat, and shook sense into her.@k@vS12/16/182200079. That is the official story.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``W-Why?! That's insane...!``
``The Grand Matriarch has sworn never to forgive the Houjou family.@k@vS12/16/182200081. She can't very well give in just because a few youngsters came begging.``

``So the narrative has to be something like: after Keiichi-kun put up a tremendous fight, the Grand Matriarch was so moved by his spirit that she made a special exception. Without a story that dramatic, it won't carry weight.@k@vS12/16/182200083. Understand, Mion?``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Mii-chan's grandmother has a lot to deal with in her position, doesn't she...``

``...Hah.@k@vS12/06/180600384. And that's how legends are born...@k@vS12/06/180600385. Now the story will be that you physically brawled with the Grand Matriarch to save Satoko.@k@vS12/06/180600386. ...People will probably cross the street when they see you coming, Kei-chan.``
``......This is the dawn of Keiichi's legend of invincibility.@k@vS12/05/180500881. Everyone will whisper and tremble and clear a path the moment they see you.``
``I-I don't need that kind of legend...!@k@vS12/01/180101520. More importantly -- ...thank you so much!``

``No need for thanks.@k@vS12/16/182200085. The town council had already decided.@k@vS12/16/182200086. We couldn't go against that even if we wanted to.@k@vS12/16/182200087. Frankly, we'd have preferred you ignore us entirely and press ahead.@k@vS12/16/182200088. It was rather underhanded of you to make us the last hurdle, Mayor Kimiyoshi.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Well... I'm just glad she agreed, all things considered...``

``The Grand Matriarch did worry about Satoko-chan, you know -- the fact that she was still being treated coldly, as if the dam war had never ended.@k@vS12/16/182200090. She thought the only chance to settle accounts would be when she herself went to her grave.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Truthfully, thanks to all of you making such a commotion, the Grand Matriarch was able to resolve this while she's still alive.@k@vS12/16/182200092. I'm sure she's grateful in her heart.``
``N-No, we're the ones who are grateful.@k@vS12/01/180101522. I'll come again another day to properly express our thanks.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Ah ha ha ha!@k@vS12/16/182200094. I'd advise against that.@k@vS12/16/182200095. Next time she really will chase you around with a katana.@k@vS12/16/182200096. Come to think of it, she didn't draw it today, did she?@k@vS12/16/182200097. She must have taken quite a liking to you, Keiichi-kun.``
``...I thought the same.@k@vS12/05/180500883. Oryou does so love her katana.``
``Rika-chan, don't say that with a smile...@k@vS12/01/180101524. I'm just grateful to be alive, honestly...``
``I've grown rather fond of you myself after today, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/16/182200099. That's how a man should be.``
``Satoko-chan is a lucky girl.@k@vS12/16/182200101. Having someone this devoted fighting for her!``
``W-Wait, Mom, Kei-chan and Satoko aren't like that...``
``Oh? They're not?@k@vS12/16/182200103. Well, if he hasn't been taken yet, you two had better step it up!``
``Hey, Keiichi-kun, pick whichever you prefer -- Mion or Shion!@k@vS12/16/182200105. You can start calling me 'Mom' from today on!``
Mion and Shion, faces blazing red, simultaneously drove a fist and an elbow into me.
...Mother or not, she was definitely a Sonozaki by blood...@k The direction and level of her jokes were exactly the same as her daughters'.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Well then, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/18/KIMI08005. You've successfully persuaded Oryou-san.@k@vS12/18/KIMI08006. As promised, the town council will support you.@k@vS12/18/KIMI08007. I'll contact the appropriate people tomorrow at noon and make our position clear.``
``After all, we have Oryou-san's backing.@k@vS12/18/KIMI08009. Honestly, just that phone call alone should be enough to get Satoko-chan rescued.``
``I-Is... Is that really...?!``
``That's how important the person you just won over is, Kei-chan.``
``Then... Satoko could really be safe by tomorrow...?!``
``It's not a certainty yet.``

``But if they refuse Mayor Kimiyoshi's request, it means they're making an enemy of the Onigafuchi Guardians.@k@vS12/06/180600389. The mayor is normally a gentle man, but when he takes up the mantle of alliance chairman -- he's a demon!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``I'll be a demon if that's what it takes.@k@vS12/18/183300104. You have my word as a man.@k@vS12/18/183300105. I'll do everything in my power to get Satoko-chan rescued as quickly as possible.``
``Thank you so much.@k@vS12/01/180101528. ...One more thing -- please stop discriminating against Satoko just because of her family name.``
``We've heard you say things like that, you know.@k@vS12/06/180600391. Calling Satoko cursed because she's a Houjou...``
``Ah... I only said those things to fit in with the others... I don't personally have anything against Satoko-chan...``
``Then stop putting on that act too.@k@vS12/02/180200652. Please accept Satoko-chan for what she truly is -- just another child of this village.``

``I understand.@k@vS12/18/183300108. I won't say anything careless again, not even over drinks.@k@vS12/18/183300109. You have my word.@k@vS12/18/183300110. I'm afraid Keiichi-kun might grab me by the collar next!``
And with that, we set off for home.
Satoko hadn't been rescued yet.@k ...But I felt we had made enormous progress.
Tomorrow, the mayor would call and formally declare his support, filing the council's appeal on our behalf.@k Naturally, with the backing of Oryou-san, the shadow ruler of the village.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``......Is Satoko... truly going to be rescued from this fate...?``

``Absolutely.@k@vS12/01/180101530. She might even be saved as soon as tomorrow!@k@vS12/01/180101531. And if that doesn't work, then we launch the full offensive.@k@vS12/01/180101532. On a scale they've never seen!!@k@vS12/01/180101533. And Rika-chan--``
``Don't throw around words like 'fate' so casually.@k@vS12/01/180101535. When you say 'fate,' it sounds like the outcome has already been decided.``

``But nothing is decided from the start.@k@vS12/01/180101537. We're the ones who create our outcomes.@k@vS12/01/180101538. So don't use the word 'fate' as if it means nothing we do can make a difference.``
Rika-chan seemed to turn my words over in her mind for a long while.
...I was starting to wonder if I'd been too harsh, carried away by the momentum -- when Rika-chan smiled.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``......If Keiichi says so, then I'll do that.@k@vS12/05/180500888. I won't use the word 'fate' so carelessly anymore.@k@vS12/05/180500889. Because you have shown me, time and again, just how easily fate can be broken.``

``......In these hundred years, even the worst possible roll of the dice -- Teppei's return -- is on the verge of being overcome,@k@vS12/05/180500891. ......so surely the inescapable fate of death must not be so formidable after all.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``......Ah, but no -- the truly worst outcome is when you never transfer to this school at all.@k@vS12/05/180500893. ...That happened once or twice in these hundred years.@k@vS12/05/180500894. Those were the loneliest worlds of all.``
``......Huh?@k@vS12/01/180101540. I really can't follow what you're saying sometimes, Rika-chan.@k@vS12/01/180101541. Are you trying to confuse me on purpose?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: If Keiichi says so, then I'll do that.@k I won't use the word 'fate' so carelessly anymore.
``Hello, is this the office? I'd like to speak with Section Chief Aida-san, please.``
``Ah, Kimiyoshi-san, the council chairman. It's been too long.@k@vS12/00/saito18004. Thank you so much for the other day -- we truly appreciated it.``
``No, no, I should be thanking you.@k@vS12/18/183300115. Really, thank you.@k@vS12/18/183300116. You were a great help.``
``Ah, the section chief just returned.@k@vS12/00/saito18006. I'll transfer you now.@k@vS12/00/saito18007. .........Section Chief, it's Kimiyoshi-san, the chairman of the Hinamizawa council.``
``Hello, hello!@k@vS12/00/183000002. What can we do for you today?``
``Well, it's about that matter with the Okinomiya child consultation center...``
